F0.rationale: Baseline hygiene remains PM/floor-owned and presence-only; secret-scan and lockfile floors exist but readme and spaces-config predicates are partial. Cannot satisfy depth.

P0.rationale: hrc-runtime is the runtime-controller layer with a single-command verify; the precondition is real but its gate is currently red and its hook unmaterialized, so P0 conditions the S6 deltas.

S1.rationale: Scope/identity parse-don't-validate is inherited from ASP and hrc-core is deliberately opaque-at-boundary; local domain invariants remain a design frontier rather than an ingress-typing requirement.

S2.rationale: ~66 Commander commands share one shape but no generator seeds it; a new-command scaffold would materially improve the construction prior.

S3.rationale: Agents enter through AGENTS.md; disclosure is reachable via the CLAUDE.md symlink but has no executable reachability guard and no package-local routers.

S3.affordanceGating.rationale: hrc-runtime is the runtime controller: it hosts sessions but does not assemble the agent prompt/context surface. Dynamic affordance-gated disclosure resides in ASP (context-template/context-resolver), so this refinement does not project onto HRC.

S4.rationale: Boundary, manifest-edge, and broker-path guards are verify-gating; every firing diagnostic should reach the fix/why/exception shape that check-cli-surface.ts already demonstrates.

S5.rationale: The repo has structural guards but no meta-lint or reviewed exception channel, so suppressions are free; a suppression-cost guard should land before the guard set grows.

S6.rationale: The local hook-enforced verify path is the handoff gate for this local-first repo; the gate must be green AND the hook materialized, and today neither holds.

S7.rationale: CLI-help and manifest-edge rot are guarded, but exported public-package surfaces can silently rot without a surface baseline sensor.

S8.note: Scored PRESENT.tacit in the assessment: taste is respected as an output, not mechanized as a process.
S8.rationale: The desired S8 output — a small, high-signal guard set with no sprawl — already holds; the authoring/cut-line procedure is deliberately tacit at this guard count and needs a written ledger only if the set grows.

TA.rationale: hrc-runtime work relies on coordinator/implementer/verifier separation socially; high-risk runtime mutation paths still lack an authority design.

TA.actuatorSplit.until: T-05439 designs and daedalus approves the HRC high-risk-lane authority split / post-approval actuator
TA.actuatorSplit.note: N=3 residency: this defer is LOCAL (the enforcement point resides here, it is simply unbuilt), NOT a cross-repo-residency strain. The only cross-repo residency edge (agent-spaces -> hrc-runtime via T-05439) is still observed from agent-spaces' end only, so the T-05443 trigger has not fired and the deferred+until+route convention stands unchallenged at N=2. See retro.
TA.actuatorSplit.rationale: hrc-runtime is the routed home for the actuator/credential split, but no design or code exists yet; the broker-path cordon is the nearest existing write-authority primitive.

TB.rationale: AGENTS.md is an active process carrier for the cross-repo publish/sync, daemon lifecycle, and hrcchat validation rituals.

TC.rationale: The repo does substantial agent work but has no enablement changelog / retro carrier, so lessons are not routed after work.

TD.rationale: A 12-package Bun/TS workspace benefits from find-entry-points / explain-area discovery beyond the CLI roster; only the roster is computed from live code today.

TD.selfDescribingSurfaceConformance.rationale: hrc + hrcchat ship large CLIs where stale help would misfire agents; hrc-runtime already meets this bar with check-cli-surface.ts and is the N=2 PRESENT corroboration of the refinement.

TE.rationale: Runtime/harness changes carry red/green TDD and integration tests as executable requirements; claim-scoped evidence routing is not yet present.

TF.rationale: hrc-runtime natively hosts an executable run/session state control plane driving live runs; TF projects onto the runtime-run domain here, not onto a task-work engine (which HRC consumes but is boundary-forbidden from importing).
